methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $97M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Nomagic is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Kacper Nowicki that has raised $97M to date. The valuation analysis relies on a comp universe of nine private robotics firms at the same stage alongside one public comparable (277810.KQ), providing benchmarks across both growth-stage private and public robotics valuations.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $97M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B progression, implying a $184M valuation. Peer-median analysis across six same-stage robotics companies yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range from $300M (Fairmat, Collaborative Robotics low-end) to $10.0B (1X Technologies). The peer set includes Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B, Dexterity at $1.6B, and other established Series B robotics entrants.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $157M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $630M. The wide dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in the peer set—particularly 1X Technologies' $10.0B valuation as an outlier—and uncertainty around Nomagic's relative technology maturity, market traction, and capital efficiency relative to named comparables. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively; peer medians suggest substantially higher valuations are supported by current market conditions in robotics.
